areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The code implements the residual building block y = F(x, {W_i}) + x (Equation 1) with parameter-free identity shortcuts for same-dimension c
The bottleneck building block for ResNet-50, ResNet-101, and ResNet-152 is implemented as a stack of three layers (1×1 conv, 3×3 conv, 1×1 c
Batch normalization (BN) is applied right after each convolution and before activation throughout all plain and residual network models, wit
Shortcut option A (zero-padding for increasing dimensions, zero extra parameters) and option B (1×1 projection convolutions with stride 2 fo
The CIFAR-10 architecture follows the 6n+2 stacked weighted layer formula with 3×3 convolutions on feature maps of sizes {32×32, 16×16, 8×8}
ImageNet training preprocessing applies scale augmentation by randomly sampling the shorter side in [256, 480], takes a 224×224 random crop
CIFAR-10 training augmentation pads 4 pixels on each side of each 32×32 image and randomly samples a 32×32 crop from the padded image or its
ImageNet comparison-study evaluation uses standard 10-crop testing; best-result evaluation uses the fully-convolutional form with scores ave
ImageNet training uses SGD with mini-batch size 256, initial learning rate 0.1 divided by 10 when the error plateaus, training for up to 60×
CIFAR-10 training uses mini-batch size 128 on two GPUs, initial learning rate 0.1 divided by 10 at 32k and 48k iterations, training terminat
ResNet-110 (n=18) training uses a warm-up phase with learning rate 0.01 until training error falls below 80% (approximately 400 iterations),
ImageNet evaluation reports both top-1 and top-5 error rates (%) using 10-crop testing on the 50k ImageNet 2012 validation set (1000-class,
CIFAR-10 evaluation reports classification error (%) on the 10k test images using a single view of the original 32×32 image; for ResNet-110
MS COCO detection evaluation reports both the PASCAL VOC metric (mAP@IoU=0.5) and the standard COCO metric (mAP@IoU=[.5:.05:.95]) on the 40k

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: On the ImageNet 2012 validation set with 10-crop testing (Table 2), the 34-layer ResNet reduces top-1 error by 3.5% compared to the plain-34
ResNet-152 achieves a single-model top-5 validation error of 4.49% on ImageNet; an ensemble of 6 ResNet models (including two 152-layer mode
On the CIFAR-10 test set (Table 6), ResNet-110 (n=18, 6×18+2=110 layers) achieves a best test error of 6.43%, and ResNet-1202 (n=200) achiev
On MS COCO validation (Table 8), replacing VGG-16 with ResNet-101 in Faster R-CNN yields a 6.0% absolute increase in mAP@[.5:.05:.95], const
The repository contains source code implementing all six ImageNet ResNet variants (ResNet-18, ResNet-34, ResNet-50, ResNet-101, ResNet-152)
The repository contains CIFAR-10 experiment code for all reported depth configurations n∈{3, 5, 7, 9, 18, 200} yielding ResNet-20/32/44/56/1

candidate.title: Fix Hardcoded /artifacts Output Directory
candidate.reasoning: Root cause of failure_class=cell_smoke_failed is unambiguous: os.makedirs('/artifacts') raises PermissionError because /artifacts is owned by root. All 20 rubric leaves score 0.0 with status=degraded_no_metrics — every zero is a downstream artifact of this single crash. Fixing it is the prerequisite for any rubric score at all.
candidate.description: Replace the hardcoded absolute path '/artifacts' in train_cell.py line 52 with a user-writable relative path (e.g., './artifacts' resolved via os.path.join(os.getcwd(), 'artifacts'), or read from an OUTPUT_DIR environment variable with a local fallback). This removes the PermissionError that killed the smoke test before any training began.

candidate.title: Enforce Paper-Exact ResNet CIFAR-10 Architecture
candidate.reasoning: Method and code fidelity carries the highest rubric weight (0.342). Five leaves directly test the residual block equation, BN placement, bottleneck design, shortcut options A/B, and the CIFAR-10 6n+2 architecture. Any deviation from the He et al. (2015) spec on these points directly costs >17% of total score even if training runs correctly.
candidate.description: Audit and correct the CIFAR-10 model definition in train_cell.py / model code to strictly implement: (a) the 6n+2 stacked-layer formula with n∈{3,5,7,9,18,200}, (b) 3×3 convolutions across feature-map sizes {32×32, 16×16, 8×8}, (c) BN immediately after every conv and before ReLU, (d) parameter-free identity shortcuts (Option A: zero-padding for dimension increases) as the default, and (e) Option B (1×1 projection with stride 2) as an explicit variant.

candidate.title: Match Paper CIFAR-10 Training & LR Schedule Exactly
candidate.reasoning: Rubric areas 'Experiment execution and reproducibility' (weight 0.154) and 'Result match versus paper targets' (weight 0.188) together account for 34% of total score. Leaf 3eae937 (CIFAR LR schedule) and 578ef601 (ResNet-110 warmup) are both currently failing. The data preprocessing leaf d8388776b (4px pad + crop + flip) is also failing. These are high-value, low-risk fixes once the code runs.
candidate.description: Harden the training loop to match the paper's CIFAR-10 protocol exactly: (a) mini-batch size 128, (b) initial LR 0.1 divided by 10 at exactly 32k and 48k iterations, (c) training terminated at 64k iterations, (d) weight decay 0.0001, momentum 0.9, (e) ResNet-110 warm-up: use LR=0.01 until training error drops below 80% (~400 iters) then switch to 0.1. Also verify the CIFAR-10 data augmentation pipeline: 4-pixel padding → random 32×32 crop → horizontal flip, per-pixel mean subtraction.

areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The code implements the residual building block y = F(x, {W_i}) + x (Equation 1) with parameter-free identity shortcuts for same-dimension c
leaves.why: train_cell.py BasicBlock.forward() implements `out = out + self.shortcut(x); out = F.relu(out)` (Eq. 2); same-dimension shortcuts use `nn.Sequential()` (parameter-free identity); dimension-changing uses option-B `nn.Conv2d(in_ch, out_ch, 1, stride=stride, bias=False)` + BN, fully

leaves.label: The bottleneck building block for ResNet-50, ResNet-101, and ResNet-152 is implemented as a stack of three layers (1×1 conv, 3×3 conv, 1×1 c
leaves.why: train_cell.py and train.py define only BasicBlock and PlainBlock (both 2-layer); no 3-layer bottleneck is present, and no ResNet-50/101/152 network class exists in any code file.

leaves.label: Batch normalization (BN) is applied right after each convolution and before activation throughout all plain and residual network models, wit
leaves.why: train_cell.py BasicBlock and PlainBlock both apply BN immediately after each conv and before ReLU (`F.relu(self.bn1(self.conv1(x)))`); CIFARNet uses `nn.init.kaiming_normal_` (He init); no Dropout layer appears anywhere in the architecture.

leaves.label: Shortcut option A (zero-padding for increasing dimensions, zero extra parameters) and option B (1×1 projection convolutions with stride 2 fo
leaves.why: train_cell.py correctly implements option A (zero-padding LambdaLayer, no parameters) and option B (1×1 Conv2d with stride), and PlainBlock uses 3×3 filters with stride-2 downsampling and global avg pool, but the FC head is 10-way only (`nn.Linear(64, 10)`) and no 1000-way ImageN

leaves.label: The CIFAR-10 architecture follows the 6n+2 stacked weighted layer formula with 3×3 convolutions on feature maps of sizes {32×32, 16×16, 8×8}
leaves.why: train_cell.py CIFARNet implements 3 stages with {16,32,64} filters and {stride=1,2,2}, 3×3 convs, n blocks per stage (2n layers), `AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)`, and `nn.Linear(64,10)`; metrics.json confirms depths 20/32/44/56/110 matching 6n+2 with n=3/5/7/9/18; all shortcuts default to

leaves.label: ImageNet training preprocessing applies scale augmentation by randomly sampling the shorter side in [256, 480], takes a 224×224 random crop
leaves.why: train_cell.py get_dataloaders() only implements CIFAR-10 preprocessing (4-pixel padding + random 32×32 crop + horizontal flip + normalize); no ImageNet data pipeline exists in any code file.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 training augmentation pads 4 pixels on each side of each 32×32 image and randomly samples a 32×32 crop from the padded image or its
leaves.why: train_cell.py get_dataloaders() applies `transforms.Pad(4, fill=0)` then `transforms.RandomCrop(32)` then `transforms.RandomHorizontalFlip()` for training; test_transform contains only `ToTensor()` + `Normalize()` — single view, no augmentation.

leaves.label: ImageNet comparison-study evaluation uses standard 10-crop testing; best-result evaluation uses the fully-convolutional form with scores ave
leaves.why: The entire codebase covers only CIFAR-10 experiments; no ImageNet validation set, no 10-crop testing, no multi-scale inference at {224,256,384,480,640} is present in train.py, train_cell.py, or any other file.

leaves.label: ImageNet training uses SGD with mini-batch size 256, initial learning rate 0.1 divided by 10 when the error plateaus, training for up to 60×
leaves.why: train_cell.py exclusively trains on CIFAR-10 with batch=128 and 64k iterations; no ImageNet training loop, no 256 mini-batch, no 60e4 iteration schedule exists in any code file.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 training uses mini-batch size 128 on two GPUs, initial learning rate 0.1 divided by 10 at 32k and 48k iterations, training terminat
leaves.why: train_cell.py sets batch_size=128, MILESTONES=(32000,48000), TOTAL_ITERS=64000, weight_decay=1e-4, momentum=0.9, He init, BN, no dropout — all matching the paper; but get_dataloaders() uses the full 50k training set, not the specified 45k/5k split.

leaves.label: ResNet-110 (n=18) training uses a warm-up phase with learning rate 0.01 until training error falls below 80% (approximately 400 iterations),
leaves.why: train_cell.py correctly implements warmup: starts at lr=0.01 when warmup_needed=True and switches to 0.1 once train_err_est<0.80; however metrics.json scope field confirms only 1 seed was run for resnet_110, not the required 5 runs.

leaves.label: ImageNet evaluation reports both top-1 and top-5 error rates (%) using 10-crop testing on the 50k ImageNet 2012 validation set (1000-class,
leaves.why: metrics.json contains only CIFAR-10 results (test_error_pct for plain/resnet models); no ImageNet top-1/top-5 metrics exist in any output file or code path.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 evaluation reports classification error (%) on the 10k test images using a single view of the original 32×32 image; for ResNet-110
leaves.why: train_cell.py evaluates test error as `1.0 - accuracy` on the 10k test set with a single view (no augmentation in test_transform), satisfying the first part; but metrics.json scope states 'ran 1 seed, paper reports mean±std over 5' — the 5-run reporting requirement for ResNet-110

leaves.label: MS COCO detection evaluation reports both the PASCAL VOC metric (mAP@IoU=0.5) and the standard COCO metric (mAP@IoU=[.5:.05:.95]) on the 40k
leaves.why: The entire codebase is restricted to CIFAR-10 image classification; no object detection code, no COCO dataset loading, no mAP computation exists in any file.

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: On the ImageNet 2012 validation set with 10-crop testing (Table 2), the 34-layer ResNet reduces top-1 error by 3.5% compared to the plain-34
leaves.why: metrics.json contains only CIFAR-10 per_model results (plain_20/32/44/56, resnet_20/32/44/56/110); no ImageNet training was performed, so the 34-layer plain vs ResNet comparison and 18 vs 34 ResNet comparison cannot be assessed from any measured metric.

leaves.label: ResNet-152 achieves a single-model top-5 validation error of 4.49% on ImageNet; an ensemble of 6 ResNet models (including two 152-layer mode
leaves.why: metrics.json contains only per_model entries for plain/resnet at depths 20–110 on CIFAR-10; ImageNet results are entirely absent, and train.py (scope section) explicitly skips all ImageNet variants.

leaves.label: On the CIFAR-10 test set (Table 6), ResNet-110 (n=18, 6×18+2=110 layers) achieves a best test error of 6.43%, and ResNet-1202 (n=200) achiev
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.resnet_110 shows best_test_error_pct=6.68% (paper claims 6.43%), and resnet_1202 is absent from per_model entirely, listed only in scope.models_skipped.

# Inspect metrics more closely

# Build final report

areas3 = rubric_result3.get("areas", [])
meets = rubric_result3.get("meets_target", False)

# Format areas for report

for a in areas3:
    report_areas.append({
        "name": a.get("area", a.get("name", "")),
        "score": a.get("score", 0.0),
        "weight": a.get("weight", 0.0),
        "notes": f"compute_adjusted={a.get('compute_adjusted_score', a.get('score', 0.0))}"
    })
